chemistry.scope_summary: Various heteroarenes (4-pyridinyl, 3-pyridinyl, 2-pyridinyl, pyrazinyl, benzothiophenyl, benzofuranyl, thiophenyl) with different R-groups (Me, Et, Ph) on allylic alcohols. Also demonstrated with different difluoroalkyl radical precursors including bromodifluorophosphonate and bromodifluoroacetamides.

optimization_table.key_findings: fac-Ir(ppy)3 with imidazole base and blue LEDs gave best results in batch, but no photocatalyst needed for flow

figures.key_information: Photochemical reaction using Ir(ppy)3 catalyst and blue LED, room temperature, 6 h or 10 min reaction time, batch and continuous flow modes, starting material prepared in flow, 15 examples in flow preparation, 19 examples total

figures.caption: Scheme 1. Scope of the Continuous-Flow Grignard Synthesis of Allylic Alcohols a | Scheme 2. Substrate Scope of the Photocatalytic Radical- Induced Heterocycle Migration − Variation of the Allylic Alcohol Substrate a

Scheme 3. Substrate Scope of the Photocatalytic Radical- Induced Heterocycle Migration − Variation of the Difluoroalkyl Radical Precursor a | Figure 1. Proposed mechanism for the photocatalytic radical-induced heterocycle migration.

translation_logic.batch_limitation: Long reaction times (6 hours) and lower yields (83%) with potential selectivity issues
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Substantial reduction in reaction time (36-fold) with improved yields and selectivity, higher concentration possible
translation_logic.what_changed: Reaction time reduced from 360 min to 10 min, concentration increased from 0.4 M to 1.0 M, yield improved from 83% to 91%
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Temperature (25°C), atmosphere (nitrogen), light source (blue LED, 12W), solvent (CH2Cl2)
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Reduced reaction time and smaller reactor volume minimize exposure to hazardous reagents

figure_descriptions: Reactor scheme showing photochemical setup with Ir(ppy)3 catalyst and blue LED
Substrate scope tables showing 15+ examples with yields 33-92%
Mechanistic scheme showing photoredox catalytic cycle with key intermediates

notes: Flow rate and throughput calculated from reactor volume (1.65 mL) and residence time (10 min). Paper demonstrates clear advantages of flow processing with 36-fold time reduction and improved yields. Optimization table shows 9 entries but specific flow optimization not detailed.
